seoTitle: SineMicro Products | Automotive MCU Power Management Distributor
seoDescription: Complete SineMicro product portfolio including automotive MCUs, power management ICs, MOSFETs, and IGBTs. Technical specifications and selection guide from authorized distributor.

faqs.question: What SineMicro product categories are available and how do I choose?
faqs.answer: SineMicro offers four main product categories: 1) Automotive MCUs - AEC-Q100 certified microcontrollers for body control, lighting, and motor drive applications. Ideal for turn signals, window controls, and seat controllers. 2) Power Management ICs - High-voltage LDOs and PMICs for automotive power supply applications. Suitable for powering sensors, MCUs, and communication modules. 3) Power Devices - Automotive-grade MOSFETs and IGBTs for power switching and motor control. Used in LED drivers, motor drives, and power distribution. 4) Integrated Solutions - MCU+Power combinations for complete system solutions. Selection depends on your specific application requirements, voltage/current needs, and system architecture. Contact our FAE team for detailed product selection guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Identify your application requirements first, then select the appropriate product category. Contact FAE for detailed selection support.

faqs.question: What is the difference between ASM87 and ASM30 MCU series?
faqs.answer: SineMicro ASM87 and ASM30 series serve different application needs: ASM87 Series - General-purpose automotive MCUs optimized for body control applications. Features include: 8-bit or 32-bit cores, integrated ADCs, PWM outputs, CAN/LIN interfaces, wide operating voltage range (2.7V-5.5V), and extended temperature range (-40°C to +125°C). Best for: turn signal controllers, window lift modules, door control modules, and seat controllers. ASM30 Series - Motor control optimized MCUs with enhanced features for motor drive applications. Features include: dedicated motor control peripherals, high-resolution PWM, integrated gate drivers, current sensing amplifiers, and advanced protection features. Best for: BLDC motor control, pump drives, fan controllers, and actuator control. Both series are AEC-Q100 qualified and share development tools for easy migration.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use ASM87 for general body control. Use ASM30 for motor control applications requiring advanced drive capabilities.

faqs.question: How do I select the right power management IC for my automotive application?
faqs.answer: Selecting SineMicro power management ICs involves several key factors: 1) Input Voltage Range - determine the system voltage (typically 12V or 24V automotive battery) and select LDOs with appropriate input voltage ratings (typically 40V-60V max for automotive). 2) Output Voltage - select fixed or adjustable output voltage based on load requirements (common: 3.3V, 5V). 3) Output Current - calculate maximum load current including margins (typically 100mA-500mA for MCU power). 4) Quiescent Current - important for standby power consumption, look for low IQ options (<100uA) for always-on applications. 5) Protection Features - ensure over-current, over-temperature, and reverse polarity protection for automotive reliability. 6) Package - select appropriate package for thermal dissipation and PCB space (SOT-223, TO-252, etc.). SineMicro offers automotive-grade LDOs with various current ratings and features to match your specific requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Define voltage, current, and protection requirements first. SineMicro LDOs offer automotive-grade reliability with competitive performance.

faqs.question: What are the key parameters for selecting SineMicro MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: Key parameters for SineMicro MOSFET selection: 1) Voltage Rating (VDS) - must exceed maximum system voltage with margin (typically 40V-100V for 12V automotive systems). 2) Current Rating (ID) - must handle maximum load current with thermal considerations (check SOA curves). 3) On-Resistance (RDS(on)) - lower is better for reduced conduction losses and heating. 4) Gate Threshold (VGS(th)) - ensure compatibility with drive voltage (typically 2.5V-4.5V for logic-level drive). 5) Switching Characteristics - consider switching frequency and requirements for Qg, Ciss for high-frequency applications. 6) Package - select based on thermal requirements and PCB space (SOP-8, DPAK, D2PAK, etc.). 7) Avalanche Rating - important for inductive load switching. SineMicro automotive MOSFETs offer low RDS(on), robust avalanche capability, and AEC-Q101 qualification for reliable automotive operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match voltage and current ratings to application requirements. Consider thermal design for high-current applications.

faqs.question: How does SineMicro ensure automotive quality and reliability?
faqs.answer: SineMicro ensures automotive quality through comprehensive processes: 1) AEC-Q100/Q101 Qualification - all automotive products undergo rigorous qualification testing including HTOL (High Temperature Operating Life), temperature cycling, ESD testing, and latch-up testing. 2) Design for Automotive - products designed from ground up for automotive requirements including wide temperature ranges, high EMC immunity, and robust protection features. 3) Manufacturing Control - products manufactured in automotive-qualified facilities with full traceability and statistical process control. 4) Quality Management - IATF 16949 and ISO 9001 certified quality management systems. 5) Field Monitoring - continuous monitoring of field performance and rapid response to any quality issues. 6) PPAP Support - complete Production Part Approval Process documentation available for customer qualification. SineMicro products have demonstrated reliable performance in millions of vehicles from major automotive OEMs.
faqs.decisionGuide: SineMicro products meet automotive quality standards. Contact us for qualification documentation and PPAP support.

faqs.question: What evaluation and development resources are available?
faqs.answer: SineMicro provides comprehensive development resources: 1) Evaluation Kits - complete evaluation boards for MCU and power product families including hardware and sample software. 2) Development Boards - feature-rich development boards with debugging interfaces, expansion connectors, and example circuits. 3) Software Tools - SineMicro Studio IDE, GCC compiler, debugging tools, and flash programming software. 4) SDK and Libraries - peripheral drivers, middleware stacks, and application examples. 5) Reference Designs - complete reference designs for common automotive applications including schematics, BOMs, and documentation. 6) Application Notes - detailed technical documentation for specific applications and design considerations. 7) Technical Training - webinars, workshops, and on-site training available. LiTong Electronics can provide evaluation kits and development resources to qualified customers. Contact our FAE team to request evaluation materials.
faqs.decisionGuide: Complete development ecosystem available. Contact LiTong for evaluation kit requests and technical training.

categories.name: Automotive MCUs
categories.description: SineMicro automotive MCUs are AEC-Q100 certified microcontrollers designed specifically for automotive body electronics applications.
categories.longDescription: SineMicro offers a comprehensive portfolio of automotive-grade microcontrollers designed for body control applications in modern vehicles. As an authorized distributor, LiTong Electronics provides complete selection guidance for SineMicro MCU families. All SineMicro automotive MCUs are AEC-Q100 Grade 1 qualified, ensuring reliable operation from -40°C to +125°C ambient temperature. The MCUs feature automotive-specific peripherals including multiple PWM channels for LED and motor control, high-resolution ADCs for sensor interfacing, and robust communication interfaces including CAN and LIN. With competitive pricing and local technical support, SineMicro MCUs provide an excellent alternative to international suppliers for automotive body electronics applications.

series.description: General-purpose automotive MCUs for body control applications

series.description: Motor control optimized MCUs with advanced drive peripherals

faqs.question: What peripherals are included in SineMicro automotive MCUs?
faqs.answer: SineMicro automotive MCUs include comprehensive peripheral sets: 1) Timers and PWM - multiple general-purpose timers and PWM channels for LED dimming and motor control. 2) ADC - 12-bit successive approximation ADC with multiple channels for sensor interfacing. 3) Communication Interfaces - CAN 2.0B controller, LIN master/slave interfaces, UART/SPI/I2C. 4) GPIO - flexible general-purpose I/O with configurable options. 5) Watchdog and Safety - independent watchdog timer, window watchdog, and clock monitoring. 6) Memory - on-chip Flash for program storage and SRAM for data.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate peripheral requirements against your application needs. Contact FAE for specific MCU recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I migrate from other automotive MCUs to SineMicro?
faqs.answer: Migrating to SineMicro MCUs involves: 1) Requirements Analysis - document current MCU features used. 2) Pin Mapping - create mapping between existing MCU pins and SineMicro equivalent pins. 3) Peripheral Configuration - review peripheral differences and adjust initialization code. 4) Code Porting - port application code to SineMicro development environment. 5) Testing - thoroughly test migrated code including functional and environmental testing. 6) Production Programming - set up production programming processes. SineMicro provides migration guides and reference code.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan migration carefully with thorough testing. SineMicro provides migration support and reference code.

products.name: Automotive 8-bit MCU with 8KB Flash
products.shortDescription: AEC-Q100 certified 8-bit automotive MCU with 8KB Flash, 512B RAM for body control applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The ASM87F0812 is a highly integrated 8-bit automotive microcontroller designed for cost-sensitive body control applications. The device features an enhanced 8051 core running up to 24MHz.
With 8KB of Flash program memory and 512B of SRAM, the ASM87F0812 provides sufficient resources for typical body control applications such as window lift modules and door control modules.

The comprehensive peripheral set includes a 12-bit ADC with 8 channels, 4 PWM channels, UART, SPI, and I2C interfaces. The device operates from 2.7V to 5.5V and is qualified to AEC-Q100 Grade 1 standards.

faeReview.author: Michael Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Automotive Electronics
faeReview.content: The ASM87F0812 is an excellent choice for entry-level automotive body control applications. This MCU hits the sweet spot of performance, features, and cost. The enhanced 8051 core provides sufficient processing power for most body control tasks while maintaining code efficiency. For customers looking to reduce BOM costs without compromising quality, the ASM87F0812 is definitely worth considering.
faeReview.insightLogic: The ASM87F0812's key advantage is the optimal balance of features and cost. For typical body control applications, the 8KB Flash and 512B RAM are sufficient. Compared to competitors' offerings, the ASM87F0812 offers similar functionality at significantly lower cost.
faeReview.highlight: Cost-effective 8-bit automotive MCU with optimal feature set for body control

alternativeParts.reason: Higher memory and I/O capacity for more complex applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications requiring more memory or I/O pins =>< Use ASM87F0812 for simpler applications

companionParts.description: 5V automotive LDO for MCU power supply

faqs.question: What is the typical development timeline for ASM87F0812 projects?
faqs.answer: Typical development timeline: 1) Hardware design - 2-4 weeks for schematic and PCB design. 2) Software development - 4-8 weeks for application code development. 3) Integration and testing - 2-4 weeks for hardware/software integration. 4) EMC and environmental testing - 2-4 weeks for automotive qualification testing. 5) Production preparation - 1-2 weeks for production programming setup. Total timeline from start to production is typically 3-6 months.
faqs.decisionGuide: Typical development timeline 3-6 months. Use reference designs and evaluation boards to accelerate development.

products.name: Automotive 8-bit MCU with 16KB Flash
products.shortDescription: Enhanced 8-bit automotive MCU with 16KB Flash, 1KB RAM for complex body control applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The ASM87F1612 is an enhanced 8-bit automotive microcontroller offering increased memory and peripheral capabilities for more complex body control applications.
Featuring 16KB of Flash program memory and 1KB of SRAM, the ASM87F1612 supports more complex application code and larger data buffers.
Extended peripherals include 6 PWM channels for multi-channel LED or motor control, additional UART and SPI interfaces, and up to 28 GPIO pins.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - MCU Applications
faeReview.content: The ASM87F1612 is my go-to recommendation for customers needing more resources than the entry-level ASM87F0812 provides. The doubled Flash and RAM capacity makes a significant difference for applications requiring complex control logic. I particularly like is the pin compatibility with the ASM87F0812 - customers can start with the smaller device and upgrade without PCB changes.
faeReview.insightLogic: The ASM87F1612 hits a sweet spot in the product line - enough resources for most body control applications without the cost of 32-bit alternatives. The pin compatibility with ASM87F0812 provides an easy upgrade path.
faeReview.highlight: Enhanced 8-bit MCU with expanded memory and peripherals for complex body control

alternativeParts.reason: Cost-optimized version for applications with lower resource requirements
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for simpler applications with lower memory and I/O requirements =>< Use ASM87F1612 for complex applications

companionParts.description: 5V automotive LDO for MCU power supply

faqs.question: What are the advantages of ASM87F1612 over ASM87F0812?
faqs.answer: Key advantages: 1) Memory - 16KB Flash vs 8KB and 1KB RAM vs 512B. 2) I/O - Up to 28 GPIO pins vs 20 pins. 3) PWM - 6 channels vs 4 channels. 4) Communication - 2 UARTs vs 1, 2 SPIs vs 1. The ASM87F1612 is ideal when your application outgrows the ASM87F0812 resources.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose ASM87F1612 when you need more memory, I/O, or peripherals. Pin-compatible with ASM87F0812 for flexible designs.

categories.name: Power Management ICs
categories.description: SineMicro power management ICs include high-voltage LDOs and PMICs designed for automotive applications with wide input voltage range and robust protection features.
categories.longDescription: SineMicro power management ICs are specifically designed for the demanding requirements of automotive electronics. As an authorized distributor, LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive selection guidance for SineMicro LDOs and power management solutions. These devices feature wide input voltage ranges to handle automotive battery voltage variations including load dump conditions, low quiescent current for always-on applications, and comprehensive protection features including over-current, over-temperature, and reverse polarity protection. All power management ICs are qualified to automotive standards with AEC-Q100 certification and operate over the full automotive temperature range of -40°C to +125°C.

faqs.question: What protection features are included in SineMicro LDOs?
faqs.answer: SineMicro automotive LDOs include comprehensive protection features: 1) Over-current protection - current limiting protects device and load from excessive current. 2) Thermal shutdown - automatic shutdown if junction temperature exceeds safe limits, with automatic restart when cooled. 3) Reverse polarity protection - protects against reverse battery connection. 4) Load dump protection - withstands automotive load dump transients up to specified voltage. 5) Short-circuit protection - current limiting during output short circuit conditions. 6) Safe operating area protection - ensures device operates within safe limits under all conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Comprehensive protection features ensure reliable automotive operation. Evaluate protection requirements for your specific application.

faqs.question: How do I select the right output current rating?
faqs.answer: Selecting LDO output current rating: 1) Calculate maximum load current - sum all load currents including MCU, sensors, and peripherals. 2) Add margin - add 20-30% margin for reliability and transient conditions. 3) Check thermal constraints - ensure package can dissipate heat at maximum current and ambient temperature. 4) Consider peak currents - some loads have high startup currents that must be accommodated. 5) Future expansion - consider future feature additions that may increase current requirements. Example: MCU (50mA) + Sensors (30mA) + Communication (40mA) = 120mA. With 25% margin: 150mA. Select 200mA or 300mA LDO for comfortable margin.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate load current with margin for reliable operation. Consider thermal constraints for high-current applications.

products.name: 300mA Automotive LDO with 5V Output
products.shortDescription: AEC-Q100 qualified 300mA LDO with 5V fixed output, wide input range up to 45V for automotive applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The SL7205 is a high-performance automotive LDO designed for powering 5V systems from automotive battery voltage. The device can handle input voltages up to 45V, accommodating load dump conditions in 12V automotive systems.

With a maximum output current of 300mA and low quiescent current of 80uA typical, the SL7205 is suitable for both active operation and always-on applications. The low dropout voltage of 350mV at 300mA ensures efficient operation even at low battery voltages.

Comprehensive protection features include over-current protection, thermal shutdown, reverse polarity protection, and safe operating area protection. The device is available in thermally enhanced SOT-223 and TO-252 packages.

faeReview.author: Lisa Wang
faeReview.title: FAE - Power Management
faeReview.content: The SL7205 is my most recommended automotive LDO for 5V MCU power applications. The 45V maximum input voltage provides excellent margin for automotive load dump conditions, and the 300mA current rating covers most MCU applications with comfortable margin. The low quiescent current of 80uA is excellent for always-on applications where battery drain is a concern. I've used this LDO in numerous automotive designs and it consistently delivers reliable performance. The protection features give peace of mind for automotive applications where robustness is critical.
faeReview.insightLogic: The SL7205 hits the sweet spot for automotive 5V LDOs - enough current for typical MCUs, wide input voltage range for automotive conditions, and low quiescent current for efficiency. The protection features are comprehensive without adding unnecessary cost.
faeReview.highlight: Automotive-grade 5V LDO with wide input range and excellent protection features

alternativeParts.reason: Higher current capability for power-hungry applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications requiring >300mA current =>< Use SL7205 for standard MCU applications
Use for 3.3V MCU and sensor applications =>< Use SL7205 for 5V systems

companionParts.description: 5V automotive MCU powered by this LDO

faqs.question: What is the dropout voltage and why does it matter?
faqs.answer: Dropout voltage is the minimum input-to-output voltage differential required for the LDO to maintain regulation. For SL7205: 350mV at 300mA load. This matters because: 1) Minimum Input Voltage - output voltage + dropout = minimum input voltage for regulation. For 5V output: 5V + 0.35V = 5.35V minimum input. 2) Battery Conditions - during cold cranking, battery voltage can drop below 6V. Low dropout ensures continued operation. 3) Efficiency - lower dropout means less power dissipation in the LDO. 4) Battery Life - less voltage headroom required extends battery life. The SL7205's 350mV dropout is excellent for automotive applications where battery voltage variations are significant.
faqs.decisionGuide: Low dropout voltage important for automotive applications with battery voltage variations. SL7205 offers excellent dropout performance.

faqs.question: How do I calculate power dissipation and thermal design?
faqs.answer: Power dissipation calculation for LDO: Pd = (Vin - Vout) × Iout + (Vin × Iq). Example: Vin = 14V, Vout = 5V, Iout = 200mA, Iq = 80uA. Pd = (14-5) × 0.2 + (14 × 0.00008) = 1.8 + 0.001 = 1.8W. Thermal design: Junction temperature TJ = TA + (Pd × θJA). For SOT-223 with θJA = 60°C/W at TA = 85°C: TJ = 85 + (1.8 × 60) = 193°C (exceeds limit). Solutions: 1) Use TO-252 package with better thermal performance, 2) Reduce input voltage if possible, 3) Reduce load current, 4) Add external heatsinking, 5) Use switching regulator for high differential applications. For high Vin-Vout differentials with high current, consider switching regulators instead of LDOs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate power dissipation and junction temperature. Use appropriate package or consider switching regulator for high differential applications.

products.name: 300mA Automotive LDO with 3.3V Output
products.shortDescription: AEC-Q100 qualified 300mA LDO with 3.3V fixed output for modern low-voltage automotive systems.
descriptionParagraphs: The SL7333 provides regulated 3.3V output for modern automotive electronics that use 3.3V logic levels. The device handles input voltages up to 45V, making it suitable for 12V and 24V automotive systems.

With 300mA output capability and low 80uA quiescent current, the SL7333 efficiently powers 3.3V MCUs, sensors, and communication modules. The 280mV dropout voltage at 300mA ensures regulation even during low battery conditions.
The device includes the same comprehensive protection features as the SL7205, ensuring reliable operation in harsh automotive environments. Available in SOT-223 and TO-252 packages.

faeReview.author: Lisa Wang
faeReview.title: FAE - Power Management
faeReview.content: The SL7333 is essential for modern automotive designs using 3.3V logic. Many newer automotive MCUs and communication ICs operate at 3.3V, and this LDO provides clean, reliable power. The lower dropout voltage compared to 5V versions is beneficial for battery-powered applications. I've successfully used this LDO in designs with 3.3V ARM-based automotive MCUs and CAN transceivers. The protection features and wide temperature range make it suitable for under-hood applications.
faeReview.insightLogic: The SL7333 fills the need for 3.3V automotive LDOs as the industry moves to lower voltage logic. The specifications mirror the popular SL7205, making it easy for customers to migrate between voltage options.
faeReview.highlight: 3.3V automotive LDO for modern low-voltage automotive electronics

comparison.Dropout: Higher dropout due to higher voltage

faqs.question: When should I use 3.3V vs 5V for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: 3.3V vs 5V selection considerations: Use 3.3V when: 1) Using modern MCUs and ICs that operate at 3.3V, 2) Power consumption is critical (lower voltage = lower power), 3) Interfacing with 3.3V sensors and modules, 4) New designs where 5V legacy compatibility not required. Use 5V when: 1) Legacy system compatibility required, 2) Using 5V sensors or actuators, 3) Higher noise immunity needed (5V has better SNR), 4) Long cable runs where voltage drop is a concern. Many modern automotive designs use mixed voltages - 5V for power devices and sensors, 3.3V for digital logic and communication. Level shifters may be needed for interfacing between voltage domains.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 3.3V for modern designs with 3.3V components. Use 5V for legacy compatibility or when higher voltage beneficial.
